sections.paragraph:
θανατηφόρος, ον (s. θάνατος, φέρω; Aeschyl., Hippocr. et al.; Diod S 3, 3, 6; 3, 5, 3; Vett. Val. 225, 7; 237, 7; 9 al.; Cyr.-Ins 9; 21; pap; LXX; TestAbr A 8 p. 86, 1 [Stone p. 20]; 17 p. 100, 1 [Stone p. 48]) death-dealing ἰός poison (SibOr, Fgm. 3, 33 p. 231 G.) Js 3:8; καρπὸς θ. (Artem. 4, 57) ITr 11:1.—M-M.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 442
